Outages & Safety

Backup electric power

An electric generator can be a valuable addition to your preparedness plan in the event of a power outage. Because generators are not connected to the power grid, they can help keep lights on and appliances operating, as well as charge important electronic devices.

Learn more about portable generators or portable power stations for your home and learn how to safely use these sources of backup power generation. 

En español

Learn about equipment types and safety

  1. Equipment overview
  2. Safety tips
  3. Additional considerations

Steps to help select backup power

A portable electric generator or a portable power station can help provide electricity to the appliances, devices and systems that you need most in an emergency. But they can also be loud, costly and potentially pose safety hazards. If you decide that a portable generator is right for you, it is essential to learn how to safely operate the device prior to an emergency. Generators also require proper storage, access to fuel and regular safety checks.

Types of generators for home use

Portable generators

Portable generators are intended to provide basic power for shorter periods of time. These generators can range in price from a few hundred dollars to well over $1,000. The size of generator you need will depend on what equipment you'd like to power. 

Permanent standby generators

Permanent standby generators are typically larger and have the potential to power an entire home. They often turn on automatically in the case of a power outage. These generators must be installed by a licensed electrician.

Portable power stations

Portable power stations are devices that operate as large, portable battery systems. They must be charged prior to an electrical outage event, although some have the ability to connect to solar panels for electricity generation. These devices can typically provide power to fewer devices than other types of generators but could serve as important additions to emergency preparedness plans.

Portable power stations

Always follow all manufacturers' instructions and heed all warnings.

Portable generators

Because generators require the storage and combustion of a fuel source, special safety measures are necessary for responsible use:

  • Always operate a generator outside at least 20 feet away from your home.
  • Never smoke near a portable generator. Always avoid having open flames near your backup power system.
  • Be sure to run your generator in a place where its exhaust can vent safely. Carbon monoxide poisoning can lead to death.
  • Turn off a gas-powered generator at least 5 minutes before refueling to allow the system to cool and prevent risk of fire.
  • To prevent overheating, use an extension cord that is the right size for your generator. The American Wire Gauge chart can be used to determine the appropriate cord.
  • Avoid running an extension cord in a high-traffic area to minimize tripping hazards.
  • Remember that a portable generator can turn off without notice, particularly if it is over-loaded.
  • If installing a permanent standby generator, be sure to hire a licensed contractor or other qualified professional for installation.

More resources

Generator Safety｜The American Red Cross | How to Prepare for Power Outages

Additional considerations

Energy source: Portable generators can run on natural gas, propane, gasoline and diesel fuel. In each case, there are emissions created when the generator is in use. Portable power stations can be charged with solar power (with the purchase of a concurrent system of solar panels) or from an electrical outlet (prior to a power outage).

Energy needs assessment: 

  • Would the generator be used to power an entire home, or just a few devices?
  • What are the essential items that would require power?
  • How much energy do each of these require? 

Type of installation:

Does your backup power source need to be portable? Permanent standby generators can provide immediate power but require professional installation and are often subject to building code requirements.

Regardless of the type of generator being used, it is critical to consult the owner’s manual for all safety requirements and instructions.

Depending on your location, operation of a generator may be subject to specific air quality regulations. 

Choose your state for equipment rebates

    1. Oregon
    2. California

    Oregon backup electric power rebate

    We’re offering eligible Oregon customers of licensed care facilities a rebate of up to $4,000, and customers who are income qualified and who rely on electricity for medical equipment a rebate of up to $1,000, on the purchase of a portable power station, battery, or permanent backup generator to support their readiness and resiliency.

    Residential rebate

    To be eligible for the residential backup power rebate program, applicants must satisfy the following requirements:

    • Must have an active Pacific Power account number as either a customer or tenant of a Pacific Power customer.

    • Must be enrolled in Pacific Power’s Medical Certificate program AND the Oregon Low Income Discount (LID) program.

    • The product purchased should be listed on the Qualified Product List and must be compliant with all local, state and federal regulatory and environmental requirements for backup electric power.

    Facility rebate

    To be eligible for the licensed care facility backup power rebate program, applicants must satisfy the following requirements:

    • Must have an active Pacific Power account number as either a customer or tenant of a Pacific Power customer.

    • Must be a licensed residential care home, facility, adult foster home or hospice.

    • The product purchased should be listed on the Qualified Product List and must be compliant with all local, state and federal regulatory and environmental requirements for backup electric power.

    • Customers with multiple facilities can contact us regarding a batch application process at PacPowerGenerator@resource-innovations.com.

       

    Rebates for this program are issued on a first-come, first-served basis each year until the maximum amount of funds is reached. 

     

    Qualified Product List

    Backup Electric Power Rebate Program Application

    Backup Electric Power Rebate Program Terms & Conditions

    En español

     

    Questions?

    Contact us at PacPowerGenerator@resource-innovations.com

    FAQ

    How do I know if I qualify for a backup electric power rebate?

    • To be eligible, you must have an active Pacific Power account number as either a customer or tenant of a customer in Oregon and:
      • Be a licensed care facility, OR
      • Be in the Medical Certificate AND Oregon Low Income Discount programs
    • Your portable power station or generator should be on the Qualified Product List and have been purchased on or after Jan. 1, 2026, and before Jan. 1, 2027.
    • Proof of purchase, in the form of a legible and dated receipt identifying the product, must be provided as a photographed image in the application portal. 

    How do I learn more and enroll in the qualifying programs to be eligible for a rebate?

    • Pacific Power’s Medical Certificate program requires an application form which includes a short description of the equipment used in the home that requires electricity and will need to be completed by a qualified medical professional — such as a Medical doctor (MD), Doctor of Osteopathy (DO), Physician Assistant (PA) or Nurse Practitioner (NP).
      • We offer an assistance program for California residential customers who may have additional energy needs related to qualifying medical conditions. California customers who have a Medical Certificate will be automatically enrolled in the Medical Baseline Program which provides a rate reduction on their bill.
      • Find more information here.

     

    • Pacific Power's Oregon Low-Income Discount (LID) program is available to income-qualified residential customers in Oregon. Eligibility and discount level are based on your annual household income (gross income) at or below 60% of Oregon state median income adjusted for household size (see below). If you qualify, you'll join more than 68,000 customers who are enrolled and getting help with their bills. 
      • Find more information here.

    What else do I need to know about participation?

    • Only one portable generator or one portable power station is eligible for the rebate program per Pacific Power meter.
    • The rebate amount cannot exceed the purchase price of the product, nor can it include taxes or shipping costs.
    • Rebate funding is available on a first-come, first-served basis until depleted. In order to be eligible for a rebate, the purchased item must be included on the Qualified Product List.

    When will I receive my rebate?

    You can expect the rebate check to arrive in the mail in about 6-8 weeks. To check on the status of your rebate application, please email PacPowerGenerator@resource-innovations.com.

    How do I know how much backup power I need?

    Oregon customers should consult a licensed electrician to help evaluate what backup power capabilities would best suit their needs. Licensed electricians can be found on Energy Trust of Oregon’s website and the State of Oregon’s website.

    1. Equipment rebates
    2. Free portable battery program

    California backup electric power rebates

    We’re offering eligible California customers a rebate on the purchase of a portable generator or portable power station to further readiness and resiliency efforts for our customers.

    Program eligibility

    To be eligible for the residential backup electric power rebate program, applicants must satisfy both of the following requirements:

    • The purchased product should be listed on the Pacific Power Qualified Product List and must be compliant with all local, state and federal regulatory and environmental requirements for backup electric power.
    • The applicant must have an active Pacific Power account number as either a customer or tenant of a Pacific Power customer.

    Rebates

    • Eligible Pacific Power customers will receive a rebate of $300 on one generator or portable power station found on the Qualified Product List.
    • Customers registered with Pacific Power in either the California Alternate Rates for Energy (CARE) or Medical Baseline programs will be eligible for an additional $500 added on to their rebate. 
    • Rebate amounts cannot exceed the purchase price of the qualified product, nor can the amount include any taxes or shipping/delivery costs.
    • Rebates for this program are issued on a first-come, first-served basis each year until the maximum amount of funds is reached. 

    Qualified Product List

    Backup Electric Power Rebate Program Application

    Backup Electric Power Rebate Program Terms & Conditions

    En español

     

    Questions?

    Contact us at PacPowerGenerator@resource-innovations.com

    How do I know if I qualify for a portable power station or generator rebate?

    • To be eligible, you must have an active Pacific Power account number as either a customer or tenant of a customer in California.

    • Customers in the Medical Baseline or California Alternative Rates for Energy (CARE) programs may be eligible for additional rebates.

    • Your portable power station or generator should be on the Qualified Product List and have been purchased on or after Jan. 1, 2026, and before Jan. 1, 2027. 

    • Proof of purchase, in the form of a legible and dated receipt identifying the product, must be provided as a photographed image in the application portal. 

    How do I learn more and enroll in the qualifying programs to be eligible for a rebate?

     
    • The California Alternate Rates for Energy (CARE) program can provide qualifying residential customers with a 25% discount on Pacific Power monthly electric bills. Simply complete the CARE enrollment form and we’ll let you know if you’re eligible. Once approved for the program, you will receive your discount for two years. After two years, you'll have to renew your application to make sure you still qualify. For households on a fixed-income, renewal is every four years. 
      • Find more information here.

     

    • Pacific Power’s Medical Certificate program requires an application form which includes a short description of the equipment used in the home that requires electricity and will need to be completed by a qualified medical professional — such as a Medical doctor (MD), Doctor of Osteopathy (DO), Physician Assistant (PA) or Nurse Practitioner (NP). 
      • We offer an assistance program for California residential customers who may have additional energy needs related to qualifying medical conditions. California customers who have a Medical Certificate will be automatically enrolled in the Medical Baseline Program which provides a rate reduction on their bill. 
      • Find more information here.

    What else do I need to know about participation?

    • Only one portable generator or one portable power station is eligible for the rebate program per Pacific Power meter. 
    • The rebate amount cannot exceed the purchase price of the product, nor can it include taxes or shipping costs. 
    • Rebate funding is available on a first-come, first-served basis until depleted. In order to be eligible for a rebate, the purchased item must be included on the Qualified Product List.

    When will I receive my rebate in the mail?

    You can expect the rebate check to arrive in the mail in about 6-8 weeks. To check on the status of your rebate application, please email PacPowerGenerator@resource-innovations.com.

    California free portable battery program

    California customers who rely on electric power for medical needs may qualify for a free portable battery. These batteries can help power medical devices, assistive technology and medical equipment during power outages. They can also help refrigerate medications.

    This program is available to residential customers who are enrolled in BOTH the Medical Baseline program and the California Alternate Rates for Energy (CARE) program. The California Medical Baseline program provides energy assistance to customers who rely on electricity to meet medical needs. The CARE program provides a discount on monthly electric bills for customers who meet certain income qualifications.

    Program eligibility

    • Have an active Pacific Power residential account in California 
    • Enrolled in the California Medical Baseline program AND enrolled in the CARE program.
    • Devices for this program are issued on a first-come, first-served basis each year until the maximum amount of funds is reached. 

    Pre-qualify to participate

    To pre-qualify for a free portable battery, please contact us toll free at 1-888-221-7070 to confirm your eligibility. 

    En español

    If I am pre-qualified, how do I get my free battery?

    1. If you are pre-qualified for the program, no action from you is needed. Our partner will contact you directly to conduct a free backup power needs assessment.
    2. Based on the assessment, you will be matched to a portable battery that best meets your needs and a battery will be delivered to you at no cost.

    Devices for this program are issued on a first-come, first-served basis each year until the maximum amount of funds is reached. 

    Why is this program targeted to medically qualified customers?

    It’s especially important that customers who rely on breathing machines, dialysis or other electric or battery-dependent medical equipment have a plan for operating these life-sustaining devices during a power outage.
